title: Cloudflare Docs Bank
intent: Compact official-doc bank for stable Cloudflare references and incoming capabilities worth tracking in this runtime.

avoid: model-by-model churn unless it changes platform shape

when_to_refresh: before broad Cloudflare architecture or capability work
when Cloudflare launches or renames a platform capability
when a beta or open beta looks operationally relevant here
when cfctl grows a surface that depends on a new official doc family
refresh_policy.operator_note: Track platform-shape changes, not every single changelog item.

docs_access.page_markdown_rule: Append /index.md to any docs page URL to fetch the Markdown version.
notes: Prefer Cloudflare's Markdown or llms.txt surfaces over HTML for agent refreshes.
Use product-specific llms.txt and llms-full.txt when a task is scoped to one product.

foundation.title: AI-friendly docs access
foundation.summary: Cloudflare publishes llms.txt, llms-full.txt, and page-level Markdown via /index.md.
foundation.why_it_matters_here: This runtime should refresh against Cloudflare's own compact text surfaces instead of scraping HTML.

foundation.title: API auth and token model
foundation.summary: API tokens remain the primary auth model; account-owned tokens remain relevant for automation and service-style use.
foundation.why_it_matters_here: This repo's lane model, token minting, and permission probes all depend on the official token model.

foundation.title: Advanced Certificate Manager edge certificates
foundation.summary: Advanced Certificate Manager can order advanced edge certificate packs for a zone, including deeper subdomain coverage, selectable CA, validation method, and validity period.
foundation.why_it_matters_here: This runtime now exposes ACM certificate-pack ordering as a preview-gated edge.certificate surface rather than ad hoc raw API calls.

sources.title: Order Advanced Certificate Manager Certificate Pack

foundation.title: Wrangler as the product runtime hinge
foundation.summary: Wrangler remains the canonical CLI surface for Workers, Pages-adjacent developer flows, bindings, env, and deployment wiring.
foundation.why_it_matters_here: This repo already wraps Wrangler heavily; incoming capability tracking should stay aligned with official Wrangler behavior.

foundation.title: Workers routes
foundation.summary: Worker route patterns connect hostnames and paths in a zone to a Worker script and are part of the hostname readiness chain.
foundation.why_it_matters_here: Hostname lifecycle verification needs live route-to-script evidence instead of source-config-only route claims.

foundation.title: API Gateway and API Shield control plane
foundation.summary: API Gateway exposes discovered operations, OpenAPI schema views, and schema-validation surfaces that are useful for API inventory and security posture.
foundation.why_it_matters_here: This runtime can use API Gateway reads to ground API-surface claims before adding or changing edge protection policy.

foundation.title: Zero Trust and Access control plane
foundation.summary: Zero Trust API and Access service tokens remain the official primitives for automation, Access apps, and service-to-service reach.
foundation.why_it_matters_here: This repo already inventories and mutates Access-adjacent state, so the official API surface remains foundational.

foundation.title: Product changelog hubs
foundation.summary: Cloudflare product changelog pages are the cleanest way to track meaningful capability movement without diffing the full docs tree.
foundation.why_it_matters_here: This bank should refresh from product changelogs first, then only expand into deeper docs when a change matters here.

foundation.title: Cloudflare's own MCP posture
foundation.summary: Cloudflare treats MCP as a first-class operating surface and publishes both general and product-specific managed MCP servers.
foundation.why_it_matters_here: This repo is an agent runtime; it should understand Cloudflare's official MCP direction even when cfctl remains the primary local interface.

watch.title: Managed Cloudflare MCP servers
watch.summary: Cloudflare's managed MCP catalog now includes the Cloudflare API MCP server and product-specific docs, bindings, and builds servers. The API server uses Codemode instead of exposing thousands of native tools.
watch.why_it_matters_here: This runtime should stay MCP-aware without becoming MCP-dependent. It is a likely future integration lane for docs and account operations.
track_here: keep cfctl docs bank aligned with Cloudflare MCP posture
consider future MCP-aware adapters only where they improve exactness instead of hiding it

watch.title: AI Search is moving fast
watch.summary: New AI Search instances now come with built-in storage and a vector index, plus namespace bindings and cross-instance search. In March 2026 Cloudflare also added public endpoints, UI snippets, and MCP support.
watch.why_it_matters_here: AI Search is no longer just a niche retrieval add-on. It is becoming a real agent-facing surface with bindings, public endpoints, and MCP.
track_here: inventory if the account starts using AI Search instances
consider future standards for public endpoint and snippet posture
track MCP endpoint usage separately from generic search usage

watch.title: Browser Rendering became Browser Run
watch.summary: Browser Rendering was renamed to Browser Run, with higher limits, a redesigned dashboard, Live View, human-in-the-loop features, session recordings, and WebMCP. The crawl API remains a beta-style surface.
watch.why_it_matters_here: This matters for agent operations, crawl posture, remote browser debugging, and any future Cloudflare-native web automation lane.
track_here: treat Browser Run as an agent/browser capability, not just screenshot tooling
watch crawl and WebMCP maturity before building first-class wrappers

watch.title: Workflows remains an active moving surface
watch.summary: Workflows continues to ship meaningful execution features such as richer step context and ReadableStream outputs. The product is already GA, but the changelog still shows a fast-moving feature surface.
watch.why_it_matters_here: This repo already inventories Workflows. The docs bank should keep their changing execution model visible without tracking every minor fix.
track_here: watch execution semantics that affect verification and operator expectations
track major language/runtime additions, not every single retry tweak

watch.title: Dynamic Workers entered open beta
watch.summary: Dynamic Workers let one Worker spin up other Workers at runtime for isolated code execution, with explicit control over bindings and network access.
watch.why_it_matters_here: This is directly relevant to agent-grade execution, code mode, and future Cloudflare-native sandboxing inside this runtime.
track_here: watch for API/runtime stabilization before adding first-class wrappers
compare against Containers and Browser Run for agent execution use cases

watch.title: Workers VPC is expanding beyond single-service bindings
watch.summary: Workers VPC added VPC Network bindings and Cloudflare Mesh support in public beta, extending Workers access deeper into private-network routing.
watch.why_it_matters_here: This is a new private-connectivity shape that may matter for future internal service access from Workers or agent tooling.
track_here: watch VPC network bindings and mesh routing maturity
treat this separately from classic Tunnel inventory

watch.title: Containers remain a real beta platform surface
watch.summary: Containers are still in beta but already support Worker-controlled images, Durable Object integration, placement, rollouts, outbound traffic handling, SSH, and R2/FUSE examples.
watch.why_it_matters_here: This repo already saw real account usage of Containers. The docs bank should keep the operational surface legible for future inventory and standards work.
track_here: watch runtime semantics, rollouts, and outbound policy changes
keep container standards distinct from Dynamic Workers and Browser Run

watch.title: Secrets Store remains important and still constrained
watch.summary: Secrets Store is still in open beta and currently integrates with Workers and AI Gateway. It is account-level, centralized, and intended to replace ad hoc secret sprawl.
watch.why_it_matters_here: This runtime should be explicit about when to use Secrets Store instead of repo env files or per-project secrets.
track_here: track product integrations beyond Workers and AI Gateway
keep standards aligned with centralized secret posture

watch.title: Pipelines is a real beta data-plane surface
watch.summary: Pipelines is still a beta surface for high-volume data ingestion with streams, SQL transforms, and R2 or Data Catalog sinks.
watch.why_it_matters_here: This is operationally relevant if the account starts using event pipelines or if R2 SQL and analytics work grows here.
track_here: watch for GA and broader sink support
keep Pipelines distinct from Queues, Logpush, and analytics exports

watch.title: API Shield vulnerability scanning is now a real API surface
watch.summary: Cloudflare's Web and API Vulnerability Scanner is in open beta via API, focused first on stateful API scanning and BOLA detection.
watch.why_it_matters_here: This is a meaningful next-step surface for security posture beyond passive API Shield inventory.
track_here: watch dashboard availability and API maturity
consider future inventory or mutation wrappers only after the surface stabilizes

watch.title: Workers Builds is still worth tracking as an incoming ops surface
watch.summary: Workers Builds remains in open beta and Cloudflare continues to raise operational limits like build disk size. Cloudflare also ships a product-specific Builds MCP server.
watch.why_it_matters_here: If this repo becomes a stronger deploy/control-plane for Worker repos, Builds may become operationally relevant here.
track_here: watch GA posture and build-management APIs
treat Builds as separate from wrangler deploy and Pages builds

excluded_noise: Do not track every Workers AI model addition here unless it changes the platform shape or control plane.
Do not track every V8/runtime patch from the Workers platform changelog here.
Do not mirror full Cloudflare changelog feeds into this repo.
